title: VS Code Agents 1.128.0-insider: Local LM Studio models no longer appear after update (Manage Language Models is empty)
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
Version:
VS Code Agents
1.128.0-insider
Commit:
a6f8e84822a159b1de1e15d98798cac65ef14f63

LM Studio:
0.4.18 (Build 1)

Problem:
After updating to 1.128.0-insider, local language models no longer appear.

This configuration worked correctly in the previous Insider version.

Reproduction:
1. Start LM Studio with OpenAI-compatible server enabled.
2. Verify http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1/models returns:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-qat",
      "object": "model",
      "owned_by": "organization_owner"
    }
  ]
}

3. Open Manage Language Models.
4. Add a Custom Endpoint:
   Base URL: http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1
   API Key: any value
5. Save.

Expected:
Gemma 4 should appear in the Language Models list.

Actual:
The endpoint is saved successfully, but Manage Language Models remains completely empty.

Additional information:
- LM Studio is reachable from the browser.
- /v1/models returns valid OpenAI-compatible JSON.
- chatLanguageModels.json also no longer loads.
- OpenAI Compatible (Deprecated) produces the same result.
- This worked before updating to 1.128.0-insider.
comments:
Additional debugging:

- Worked correctly before updating to 1.128.0-insider.
- Same installation and same Windows user profile.
- LM Studio 0.4.18 (Build 1).
- GET http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1/models returns:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "gemma-4-26b-a4b-it-qat",
      "object": "model",
      "owned_by": "organization_owner"
    }
  ],
  "object": "list"
}

- Creating a Custom Endpoint succeeds with no errors.
- Creating an OpenAI Compatible (Deprecated) endpoint also succeeds.
- Both endpoints appear to save successfully.
- "Manage Language Models" remains empty.
- Existing chatLanguageModels.json is ignored.
- Restarting the application and reloading the window does not help.

Hi @Parsa-Mah I tried reproducing this but wasn't able to with this in my chatLanguageModels.json in today's insiders: ``` { "name": "LM Studio", "vendor": "customendpoint", "apiType": "chat-completions", "models": [ { "id": "deepseek-r1-0528-qwen3-8b-mlx", "name": "deepseek-r1-0528-qwen3-8b-mlx", "url": "http://127.0.0.1:1234", "toolCalling": true, "maxInputTokens": 31072, "maxOutputTokens": 31072 } ] } ``` ``` Version: 1.128.0-insider (Universal) Commit: 469425b939f7925426af3f39533864f7530e45bb Date: 2026-07-02T13:10:11Z Electron: 42.5.0 ElectronBuildId: 14525058 Chromium: 148.0.7778.271 Node.js: 24.17.0 V8: 14.8.178.33-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 25.5.0 ``` Could you try the following 1. Set Developer log level to trace and restart VS Code 2. open the `Output` view, and see if you notice any errors in the `Github Copilot Chat`, `Main`, `Window`, or `Extension Host` channels. 3. Provide the error if you do see any

After updating to commit 469425b939f7925426af3f39533864f7530e45bb, the Agents launcher button disappeared from the title bar on Windows. Previously it appeared between Customize Layout and Search. Is this expected? If not, could this indicate that my installation is no longer running the same Agents experience you're testing?

I discovered that the built-in GitHub Copilot extension had been disabled globally. Re-enabling it restores (or does not restore) the Agents UI and local models. Agents button is back but Disabling the built-in GitHub Copilot extension removes the Agents UI and also disables local language model support.

And On Windows, which chatLanguageModels.json location is currently used by Agents?

%APPDATA%\Code - Insiders\User\

or another user data directory?

Also I found another clue.

In both chatLanguageModels.json files, VS Code now reports:

Value is not accepted. Valid values: .

on the "vendor": "customendpoint" property.

Additionally:

Developer: Show Running Extensions shows no AI/Chat/Language Model related extensions.
The Agents button that previously appeared in the title bar disappeared after updating.
Manage Language Models is empty.

This makes me suspect the Agents/Chat components are not loading at all on my Windows installation, rather than there being a problem with LM Studio itself.

I found another important clue.

After re-enabling the built-in GitHub Copilot extension:

the Agents button returned,
customendpoint is no longer marked as invalid in chatLanguageModels.json.

However, the issue persists.

I checked the LM Studio server logs while opening Manage Language Models.

VS Code Agents never sends any request to LM Studio.

The only GET /v1/models in the log is from my web browser when I manually visited the endpoint.

There is no request from VS Code to:

GET /v1/models
POST /v1/chat/completions

So it appears the custom endpoint provider is never instantiated, rather than failing to parse the response.

On Windows 1.128.0-insider, the custom endpoint provider never attempts to connect to LM Studio, even though the configuration is valid and the Agents infrastructure is active.

Since VS Code never issues a request to /v1/models, is there a trace flag or output channel specifically for custom endpoint registration or the language model service? The existing Output channels don't show any activity related to custom endpoints.

I found the root cause.

It turns out this was not an LM Studio or chatLanguageModels.json issue.

I'm located in Iran, where GitHub Copilot and some GitHub AI services are unavailable due to sanctions. I enabled a system-wide VPN, restarted VS Code Insiders, and immediately:

Local language models appeared in Manage Language Models.
VS Code successfully connected to LM Studio.
The custom endpoint worked as expected.

So it seems that even though I'm only using a local model via a Custom Endpoint, some initialization or validation step still depends on reaching GitHub/Copilot services. Without network access to those services, the local models are never discovered or shown, and no request is sent to the local LM Studio endpoint.

If this dependency is intentional, it might be helpful to surface an error explaining why local models are unavailable. If it's not intentional, it may be worth investigating whether Custom Endpoints can initialize independently of GitHub/Copilot connectivity.

Thanks for your help!
